evals.name: eval-vitest-statusline
evals.prompt: I have a Node.js project that uses vitest for testing. I want to see real-time test progress in my Claude Code status bar — like a progress bar that shows how many tests passed/failed while they're running. Can you help me build this?
evals.expected_output: Should provide the full 8-step workflow: detect vitest, scaffold extension, implement render script (CJS), implement wrapper with adapter, configure extension.json with statusLine + hooks, mention coexistence option. Should reference examples like minimal-render.cjs and minimal-wrapper.cjs.

assertions.name: mentions-cjs-requirement
assertions.text: Output mentions that scripts must be CommonJS (.cjs) format because Claude Code runs them via node

assertions.name: mentions-yaml-status-file
assertions.text: Output mentions YAML status file as the communication mechanism between wrapper and render

assertions.name: mentions-atomic-writes
assertions.text: Output mentions atomic writes (temp file + rename) for YAML status updates

assertions.name: mentions-session-id
assertions.text: Output mentions session_id or session prefix for file isolation

assertions.name: provides-render-script
assertions.text: Output includes or references a render script that reads YAML and outputs ANSI progress bar

assertions.name: provides-wrapper-script
assertions.text: Output includes or references a wrapper script that spawns test command and writes YAML

assertions.name: mentions-extension-json
assertions.text: Output mentions configuring extension.json with statusLine command and hooks

assertions.name: vitest-specific
assertions.text: Output includes vitest-specific guidance (adapter patterns, npx vitest run command)

evals.name: eval-coexistence-wrapper
evals.prompt: I already have ccstatusline installed in my Claude Code (shows git branch and time). Now I want to add test progress to the statusline too, but I don't want to lose the ccstatusline output. How do I make both work together? I'm on Windows.
evals.expected_output: Should focus on Step 7 (Statusline Coexistence). Should explain the wrapper pattern: detect existing statusline, base64 encode both commands, create wrapper that runs both sequentially with timeout, combine output with separator. Should mention Windows-specific concerns (windowsHide, path.join with os.homedir).

assertions.name: explains-single-statusline-limitation
assertions.text: Output explains that Claude Code supports only one statusLine command, hence the need for a wrapper

assertions.name: mentions-base64-encoding
assertions.text: Output mentions base64 encoding of commands to avoid shell escaping issues

assertions.name: mentions-wrapper-script
assertions.text: Output describes or provides a wrapper script that runs both commands

assertions.name: mentions-timeout
assertions.text: Output mentions timeout handling for wrapper commands (e.g. 5s timeout)

assertions.name: mentions-output-combining
assertions.text: Output describes combining outputs (e.g. with separator like ' | ')

assertions.name: windows-aware
assertions.text: Output includes Windows-specific guidance (windowsHide, portable paths, or cross-platform mentions)

assertions.name: detects-existing-statusline
assertions.text: Output mentions detecting the existing statusline from ~/.claude/settings.json

evals.name: eval-pytest-adapter
evals.prompt: I'm building a test statusline extension and I already have the render script and wrapper working. Now I need to parse pytest output line by line to get real-time test progress instead of just pass/fail at the end. What's the adapter pattern I should follow?
evals.expected_output: Should reference adapter-patterns.md. Should explain the parseLine(line) -> TestEvent interface, TestEvent types (test_pass, test_fail, suite_start, etc.), and how to write regex patterns for pytest output format. Should mention the dispatch table for pytest command.

assertions.name: explains-adapter-interface
assertions.text: Output describes the parseLine(line) -> TestEvent | null adapter interface

assertions.name: lists-test-event-types
assertions.text: Output lists TestEvent types (at least test_pass, test_fail, suite_start)

assertions.name: pytest-specific-patterns
assertions.text: Output includes pytest-specific stdout patterns or regex examples

assertions.name: mentions-wrapper-integration
assertions.text: Output explains how the adapter integrates with the wrapper (pipe stdout lines through parseLine)

assertions.name: mentions-dispatch-table
assertions.text: Output mentions or references the dispatch table with pytest command and filter flag
